subject.title: Spanish ab initio
subject.description:
IB Spanish ab initio is a Group 2 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). Designed for students with little to no prior experience in Spanish, this beginner-level language acquisition course develops basic communicative competence across speaking, listening, reading, and writing skills. The course explores themes including identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet, while fostering intercultural understanding and effective communication in diverse Spanish-speaking contexts.

The course is available at Standard Level only (SL, 150 teaching hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (Writing tasks of 70-150 words: SL 1 hour, 25%), Paper 2 (Listening and reading comprehension with unseen texts: SL 1 hour 45 minutes, 50%), and Internal Assessment (Individual oral examination based on visual stimulus with follow-up discussion: SL 7-10 minutes, 25%).

strengths: Title and introduction communicate the focus on allegory and violence with precision and appropriateness
Research question is clearly stated, focused on a single literary device, and consistently linked to analysis
Theoretical framework defines key terms (gÃ³tico latinoamericano, alegorÃ­a) providing a solid analytical foundation
weaknesses: Introduction contains some redundancy and relies on plot summary before foregrounding the research question
Methodology lacks breadth in secondary scholarship, relying heavily on nonâpeerâreviewed sources
Citation style is occasionally irregular, indicating only partially informed selection of sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation communicates its topic and purpose clearly and effectively, with a focused research question that remains connected throughout the essay, meriting a mark in the 5-6 band. However, minor repetitiveness in the introduction and a methodology that, while workable, shows only partial evidence of informed source selection place the criterion just below the top mark at 5.

strengths: Frequent, pertinent quotations from primary texts are well integrated into the argument about allegory
Subject knowledge of Ojedaâs work, genre context, and literary conventions is clear and coherent
Discussion of tone and claustrophobia demonstrates perceptive linking of diction to atmosphere
weaknesses: Occasional quotations lack inâdepth analysis and some passages rely too heavily on plot summary
Terminology use is mostly accurate but sometimes imprecise (e.g., âcoÃ¡guloâ equated simplistically with trauma)
Dependence on dictionary and nonâacademic sources limits demonstration of scholarly engagement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay applies source material in a mostly relevant and appropriate way and shows clear, coherent subject knowledge, placing it securely in the 3-4 band. Inconsistencies in terminology accuracy and limited use of peer-reviewed scholarship prevent elevation to the top band, yielding a final score of 4.

strengths: Primaryâtext analysis remains consistently relevant to the research question, with wellâchosen quotations supporting interpretation
Analysis of symbols (e.g., brujas, coagulaciÃ³n) is perceptive and generally logical
Arguments about narrative voice, tone, and structure are well developed and grounded in textual evidence
weaknesses: Secondary research is limited, and there is little engagement with critical theory to strengthen arguments
Discussion lacks systematic evaluation of alternative interpretations or explicit consideration of limitations
A few analytical sections are incomplete, and some claims are asserted without full evidentiary support
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates good research quality and depth of analysis, with a coherent, reasoned argument throughout. While the argument is strong, scholarship depth and explicit critical evaluation are moderate rather than extensive, positioning the work in the upper end of the 4-9 band. A score of 10 reflects solid critical thinking that falls just short of excellence in the highest band.

strengths: Structure is clearly appropriate and complete, with logical progression from theory to analysis and a comprehensive table of contents
Headings are consistently styled, guiding the reader effectively through sections
The layout of title page, contents, chapters, conclusion, and bibliography is coherent and user-friendly
weaknesses: Page numbers are absent or not clearly visible, which may impede navigation
Some bibliographic entries are irregularly formatted
Minor inconsistencies in chapter length and small duplications in the contents list disrupt flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs overall structure and layout meet the criteria for a clearly appropriate and complete presentation. Small lapses in pagination and bibliographic formatting prevent perfection, but the work nonetheless fulfills the 3-4 band descriptors, justifying the maximum mark of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question â âÂ¿CÃ³mo MÃ³nica Ojeda utiliza la alegorÃ­a para comunicar temas de violenciaâ¦?â â is stated verbatim on the title page, focused on a single technique (allegory) within one work, and is closely revisited in each analytical subsection and the conclusion.  Its wording and scope are neither too broad nor too narrow and remain consistently linked to the discussion.  This alignment satisfies the 5-6 band; some repetition of the question inside the body marginally limits sophistication, so a 5 rather than 6 is awarded.

Analysis moves beyond summary to interpret how specific symbols (brujas, coÃ¡gulos, umas, caninos) convey violence, and it links narrative voice, tone and structure to meaning.  Arguments are generally logical, yet some claims are asserted without full evidentiary support and counter-argument is absent.  The work is therefore âgoodâ but not âexcellentâ, meriting a solid 5 in the 4-6 band.

The essay incorporates numerous pertinent quotations from the three stories and usually links them to the argument about allegory.  Secondary references (dictionary, newspaper article, academic webpage) are used mainly for definitions, yet remain relevant.  Occasional quotation without analysis and the absence of critical scholarship narrow the effectiveness, placing the work solidly in the âmostly relevant and appropriateâ (3-4) band, awarded 4 marks.

The essay includes a word count, title, table of contents and bibliography.  Headings are consistently styled and sections separated.  However, there is no explicit evidence of page numbering in the extract, and some bibliographic entries are irregularly formatted.  Hence layout is largely correct but not flawless, fitting the 3-4 band; 3 marks are appropriate.

The student shows sound knowledge of Ojedaâs plots, the concept of allegory and features of the Latin-American Gothic.  Definitions are accurate and explanations coherent.  Some over-reliance on plot summary and minor conceptual simplifications (e.g., treating every symbol as allegory) prevent the work from reaching the consistently sophisticated level of the top band.  Overall clarity and coherence justify the upper end of 3-4.

A logical structure (title page, contents, chapters, conclusion, bibliography) is provided and consistently followed.  Headings guide the reader and the progression from theory to analysis is clear, fulfilling the descriptor of a âclearly appropriate and completeâ structure.  Minor duplication in the contents list and uneven chapter lengths are insignificant; the maximum band score (4) is justified.

Primary-text research is adequate and clearly related to the question; each story is analysed with multiple textual references.  Secondary research is limited but still supports definitions and contextual framing.  Relevance is maintained throughout, though depth of scholarship is moderate rather than extensive.  These qualities align with the middle band (4-6); balanced strengths warrant 5 marks.

The studentâs title, abstract sentence and introduction communicate a precise literary focus: the use of allegory to portray violence in three specified stories of Las voladoras.  The thematic scope (abortion, parricide, abuse) is entirely appropriate for a Category 1 Spanish-literature EE and is made explicit for the reader.  Minor language slips do not impede communication.  Because the purpose, context and relevance are all clear, the strand meets the descriptors for the 5-6 band, though slight redundancy in the introduction prevents the very highest quality.

The essay is organised thematically and ends with a conclusion that revisits the research question.  Reasoning is generally coherent, though evaluative commentary on alternative readings or limitations is scarce.  Occasional repetition and descriptive passages weaken the overall persuasiveness.  These features place discussion at the lower end of the âgoodâ band; 4 marks are assigned.

A brief âMarco teÃ³ricoâ defines key concepts (gÃ³tico latinoamericano, alegorÃ­a, parricidio, etc.) and the contents page signals a clear plan.  Primary material is the short-story collection; seven secondary/lexical sources are listed and cited.  While this constitutes a workable methodology, the scholarly range is thin (no peer-reviewed criticism on Ojeda, limited theoretical voices) and citation style is sometimes irregular, so informed selection is only partially demonstrated.  Hence the strand fits comfortably in the 3-4 band, at the upper edge.

Literary terminology such as âalegorÃ­aâ, ânarrador omniscienteâ, âanalepsisâ, âmetamorfosisâ is employed largely correctly, showing basic conceptual grip.  Nonetheless, imprecision appears (e.g., equating âcoÃ¡guloâ directly with trauma, occasional misuse of âsexualidad rojaâ), and consistency wavers.  Therefore the work meets but does not exceed the âmostly accurateâ threshold; 3 marks are appropriate within the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly articulated and precisely focused on allegory within the chosen text, maintaining a strong link to the subsequent analysis.
The title succinctly conveys the literary focus on allegory and violence, establishing a clear and appropriate purpose for the investigation.
The heading style in the contents page is consistent, but the page-range format (e.g., â5-14â) could be replaced with specific start and end pages for clarity.
There is no visible page number in the footer, which may impede navigation; consider adding pagination to meet layout requirements.
The introduction effectively situates the author and work, showing clear subject knowledge of context and genre conventions.
The inclusion of the word count demonstrates adherence to formatting requirements and ensures the work stays within the prescribed limit.
The table of contents is clearly structured and comprehensive, guiding the reader effectively through the sections of the essay.
The theoretical framework clearly defines âgÃ³tica latinoamericanaâ and âalegorÃ­a,â establishing a solid basis for analysis.
Consider integrating peerâreviewed scholarship on allegory and Latin American Gothic to deepen your theoretical discussion and demonstrate informed source selection.
The opening paragraph on the allegory of abortion is descriptive but would benefit from more critical interpretation of how symbols function in narrative structure.
Good use of a direct quotation to support analysis of narrative perspective and its emotional impact.
The discussion of tone and claustrophobia demonstrates perceptive literary analysis linking diction to atmosphere.
The inâtext citation for the quote is missing a closing parenthesis and page number formatting is inconsistent.
The spelling âfluveâ is incorrect; revise to âfluyaâ or a more precise term to maintain academic clarity.
The introduction relies heavily on plot summary; focus on foregrounding the research question earlier to sharpen the essayâs focus and avoid redundancy.
The chapter heading clearly guides the reader into the analytical section, reinforcing a logical structure.
The interpretation of âcoÃ¡gulosâ as trauma effectively unpacks symbolic meaning, demonstrating solid critical thinking.
The causal link between coagulation and trauma is insightful, but consider citing critical theory on bodily symbolism to reinforce your argument.
The bibliography includes a newspaper article; consider expanding with academic journal sources to demonstrate scholarly engagement.
The analysis of the songâs repetitive sounds as allegory is perceptive and adds a rich layer to your interpretation.
The subheading â3.2 AlegorÃ­a del parricidioâ clearly demarcates the next analytical focus, enhancing navigability.
This paragraph outlines themes but lacks specific quotations; integrate direct evidence to substantiate claims about the parricidio allegory.
The dual narration discussion demonstrates nuanced understanding of perspective shifts and their emotional effect.
The paragraph on gÃ³tico latinoamericano ends midâsentence, disrupting coherence; revise to complete the thought and maintain flow.
The interpretation of Umas as a reflection of psychological state is wellâargued and grounded in textual evidence.
The subheading for the third case study is clear and maintains structural consistency across analyses.
The analysis of âCaninosâ begins but is cut off, suggesting incomplete development; ensure full explanations are included.
The synthesis of analepsis, irony, and metamorphosis demonstrates clear subject knowledge and links devices to thematic depth.
Excellent identification of irony in the text and its reversal of roles, showcasing analytical insight.
The LaTeX math snippet appears erroneously in the text and disrupts readability; remove or correct its placement.
The analysis of metamorphosis insightfully links physical change to character agency and thematic meaning.
The home-as-prison allegory is wellâdeveloped, linking domestic imagery to psychological entrapment convincingly.
The paragraph on âinstinto primitivoâ ends abruptly; expand the argument and link back to the research question for coherence.
The interpretation of dehumanization through the âcaninoâ allegory is compelling and wellâsubstantiated.
The conclusion reiterates key findings but would benefit from evaluating limitations or suggesting alternative interpretations.
Reliance on a Babelio entry is problematic; consider substituting with peerâreviewed criticism to strengthen academic rigor.
The date and numbering style in this citation is inconsistent with academic conventions; standardize format across all entries.
The primary text citation is correctly formatted, reflecting attention to bibliographic standards.
Ensure retrieval dates are provided for all online sources to maintain consistency and allow verification.
The title of this reference is incomplete and trailing; verify the full title and author details to ensure accuracy.
